chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Modbus Organization partnership with Rheonics

Modified on: Wed, 28 Feb, 2024 at 10:56 PM

The Modbus Organization is a group of independent users and suppliers of automation devices that seeks to drive the adoption of the Modbus communication protocol suite and the evolution to address architectures for distributed automation systems across multiple market segments. The Modbus Organization will also provide the infrastructure to obtain and share information about the protocols, their application, and certification to simplify implementation by users resulting in reduced costs.

Modbus RTU and Modbus TCP/IP are available on Rheonics density meters and viscometers over a 2-wire RS-485 interface and Ethernet port.

Modbus ready density and viscosity meter

Outputs:
Density
Viscosity
Temperature
Concentration
Compensated viscosity
Compensated density

Figure 1. RS485 and Ethernet port used for Modbus communication

1. Rheonics Membership

Rheonics is a member of the Modbus organization as a vendor of hardware, software, and Modbus-compatible systems. Check our membership at this link: Modbus Organization Members (https://modbus.org/about.php)

chunks.page_summary: This page details Rheonics' Modbus integration capabilities, support resources, and their membership in the Modbus organization for their fluid process monitoring instruments.
chunks.extracted_text:

Rheonics brings together a team of experts from the leading universities and global companies to build cutting edge fluid process monitoring instruments. Rheonics is advised by an advisory board made of leaders from the industrial automation & process industry.
Figure 2. Rheonics membership in the Modbus organization
Rheonics provides detailed user manuals and technical support for integration. Our Modbus-compatible sensors can be easily integrated into Modbus networks thanks to all the guidelines and steps provided in our support portal. In addition, our experienced engineering team can assist with installation and configuration to ensure successful integration into your Modbus network on time. Visit our Support portal for more information on Modbus Protocol: Modbus RTU (RS-485) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) and Modbus TCP/IP (Ethernet) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217062).
Figure 3. Modbus partnership between Modbus organization and Rheonics
2. How does this partnership benefit your process?
When evaluating network options for your device, both Modbus TCP/IP and Modbus RTU provide distinct advantages:

chunks.page_summary: This page details the implementation and benefits of Modbus communication in Rheonics sensors, outlines various applications where Modbus is utilized by Rheonics customers, and describes advanced calculation models available via Modbus.
chunks.extracted_text:
with a common speed being 9600 baud, indicating the number of bits transmitted per second.

In Standard Modbus, information is organized and stored within the Server device using four distinct tables. These tables are designed to accommodate different types of data – two for on/off discrete values (coils) and two for numerical values (registers). Importantly, each of these tables comprises both a read-only section and a read-write section.

5. Why do we have this option in our sensors?

In multiple automation applications, Modbus plays a pivotal role by ensuring smooth communication between an array of field devices and the central control system, with a special emphasis on its integration with PLCs.

Rheonics SME with Modbus communication facilitates remote access to comprehensive device diagnostics and configurations, resulting in swift decision-making, reduced downtime, and an overall enhancement of system efficiency.

6. Rheonics Expertise

A few typical applications where our customers are using Modbus protocol are:

*   Transmission of density (weight) of drilling mud over Modbus.
*   The viscosity of batter mix that is applied to various appetizer food products.
*   Plywood glue manufacturing and application - measure and transmit viscosity and temperature of glue.
*   Measure and transmit viscosity in the process for water-based ink.
*   Measure and transmit the density, viscosity, and temperature of ceramic slurry from the slurry drum to the PLC and SCADA system in shell rooms.
*   Monitoring and transmission of density, viscosity, and temperature to SCADA system over Modbus in Processing plants for food production as well as in the beverage industry and dairies
*   Automation and control for water/wastewater treatment uses PLC with Modbus communication to get sewage water density and viscosity for nitrification, polyelectrolyte, and calamity detection.
*   and many more.

7. What is available in Modbus?

We also offer advanced calculation models for compensated viscosity, compensated density, and concentration which can be transmitted over Modbus along with the line measurements. Learn how you can get °API, °Baume, compensated viscosity, and density using the onboard MATHS engine > Measurements (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/81000186361)

8. Resources

1. Modbus RTU (RS-485) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005)
key_facts: Standard Modbus organizes information in a Server device using four distinct tables: two for on/off discrete values (coils) and two for numerical values (registers).
Each Modbus table includes both a read-only section and a read-write section.
Modbus plays a pivotal role in automation applications by ensuring smooth communication between field devices and central control systems, with a special emphasis on PLCs.
Rheonics SME sensors with Modbus communication facilitate remote access to device diagnostics and configurations.
Modbus communication in Rheonics SME sensors leads to swift decision-making, reduced downtime, and an overall enhancement of system efficiency.

Rheonics customers use Modbus for various applications including transmission of density for drilling mud, viscosity of batter mix, viscosity and temperature of plywood glue, viscosity of water-based ink, and density, viscosity, and temperature for ceramic slurry, food production, beverage industry, dairies, and water/wastewater treatment.
Rheonics offers advanced calculation models for compensated viscosity, compensated density, and concentration transmittable via Modbus.
The onboard MATHS engine can provide values such as °API, °Baume, compensated viscosity, and density.
